agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive delivery with a 2-hour window, premium pricing ($12.99 surcharge), and a refund policy for missed windows. Targets customers within proximity of warehouse locations. Discovered via cross-functional workshop with PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery Fulfillment — covers order eligibility, driver assignment, SLA tracking, and refund adjudication for the express tier. Touches Order, Driver, Warehouse, and Billing contexts.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery tier promising fulfillment within a 2-hour window from order placement, subject to eligibility constraints." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "A $12.99 surcharge applied on top of the base delivery fee for express-tier orders." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge that applies to all orders regardless of tier. Not refundable under the express SLA miss policy." },
      { "term": "Express Eligibility", "definition": "The set of preconditions a customer/order must satisfy to select express: geographic proximity to a warehouse (within 10km) and availability of a qualified driver." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute window from order placement within which a driver must physically pick up the package from the warehouse." },
      { "term": "Delivery Window", "definition": "The 2-hour period measured from order placement within which the express delivery must be completed." },
      { "term": "Express-Qualified Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of at least 4.0 stars who is eligible to fulfill express orders." },
      { "term": "SLA Miss", "definition": "An event where the delivery is not completed within the 2-hour delivery window, triggering potential refund adjudication." },
      { "term": "Refund Adjudication", "definition": "The process of determining whether an SLA miss qualifies for a goodwill express fee refund, considering factors such as address accuracy." },
      { "term": "Driver Cancellation Replacement Window", "definition": "The 10-minute window after a driver cancels mid-delivery within which a replacement driver must be assigned." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "A non-contractually-obligated refund of the express fee issued when the delivery window is missed through no fault of the customer." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address Fault", "definition": "A determination that the customer provided an incorrect delivery address, which disqualifies them from receiving an SLA-miss refund." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A customer may only select Express Delivery if their delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — beyond 10km the 2-hour window is operationally infeasible. Allowing it would create un-fulfillable orders.",
      "testable_assertion": "An order with a delivery address >10km from the nearest warehouse MUST be rejected for express tier at order placement time."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing is a business policy that Finance or Product could adjust. The amount is not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "When an express order is placed, the total includes exactly the base delivery fee + $12.99 express surcharge."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "An express order must be picked up from the warehouse within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operations states the 2-hour SLA is infeasible if pickup exceeds 15 minutes. This is a physical/operational constraint that protects the integrity of the delivery window.",
      "testable_assertion": "If no driver picks up the order within 15 minutes, the system MUST escalate (reassign, alert ops, or flag the order)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or higher may be assigned to express orders.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Quality gate protecting service reliability. Assigning a lower-rated driver to a time-critical delivery violates the operational contract.",
      "testable_assertion": "The driver assignment algorithm MUST filter out drivers with rating < 4.0 for express orders. An express order MUST NOT be assigned to a sub-4.0 driver."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When an SLA miss occurs and the customer is not at fault (e.g., wrong address), the express fee ($12.99) is refunded as a goodwill gesture. The base delivery fee is never refunded under this policy.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Refund policy is a business/goodwill decision that management or legal could adjust. Not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an SLA miss where wrong_address_fault = false, a refund of exactly the express fee amount is created. The base delivery fee is excluded from the refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No express fee refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Policy-level exclusion. Could be changed by CS or Legal. Not a data integrity rule.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an SLA miss where wrong_address_fault = true, no refund is created and the reason 'customer_address_fault' is recorded."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "SLA-miss refunds are processed within 3 business days of the miss event.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Processing SLA is a Finance operations policy, not a system invariant.",
      "testable_assertion": "A refund record created from an SLA miss has a processing_deadline <= 3 business days from the miss timestamp."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational target, not a physical constraint. There may be no available drivers, making this a best-effort policy.",
      "testable_assertion": "When a driver cancellation event occurs on an active express delivery, the system initiates replacement assignment and tracks a 10-minute deadline."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "If no replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes of a mid-delivery cancellation, an automatic refund of the express fee is triggered.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Automatic refund trigger is a CS policy decision, not a data integrity rule.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation event where replacement_assigned_within_10min = false, a refund is auto-created without manual intervention."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-10",
      "rule": "Express customers must have access to real-time driver tracking from the moment a driver is assigned until delivery completion.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "UX requirement, not a system integrity constraint. Tracking outage doesn't corrupt data.",
      "testable_assertion": "When an express order transitions to 'driver_assigned' state, a real-time tracking session is created and location updates are emitted at defined intervals."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A customer with a delivery address 8km from the nearest warehouse",
      "when": "They select Express Delivery at checkout",
      "then": "The order is accepted as express, the $12.99 express fee is added, and a 2-hour delivery window is set from order placement time"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A customer with a delivery address 11km from the nearest warehouse",
      "when": "They attempt to select Express Delivery",
      "then": "The express option is unavailable or rejected with a clear message explaining the distance constraint"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "An express order has been placed",
      "when": "No driver picks up the package within 15 minutes",
      "then": "The system escalates the order (ops alert, reassignment attempt) and the pickup_sla_breached event is recorded"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "An express order where the delivery is completed at 2 hours and 5 minutes after placement, and the customer address was correct",
      "when": "The system evaluates the SLA",
      "then": "An SLA miss is recorded, the express fee refund is queued, and the base delivery fee is NOT refunded"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "An express order where delivery is late but the customer provided a wrong address",
      "when": "The system evaluates the SLA miss for refund eligibility",
      "then": "No refund is issued and the reason 'customer_address_fault' is logged"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order",
      "when": "10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver being assigned",
      "then": "The system automatically triggers an express fee refund without requiring manual approval"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order",
      "when": "A replacement driver is assigned within 10 minutes",
      "then": "The delivery continues under the original 2-hour window (clock does NOT reset) and no automatic refund is triggered"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "An express order with an assigned driver",
      "when": "The customer opens the order tracking screen",
      "then": "Real-time driver location is displayed with live updates"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "The express driver assignment algorithm is selecting a driver",
      "when": "Available drivers include ones rated 3.8 and 4.2 stars",
      "then": "Only the 4.2-star driver is eligible for assignment; the 3.8-star driver is excluded"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10.0km from the nearest warehouse", "expected_behavior": "AMBIGUOUS — spec says 'within 10km' but doesn't clarify inclusive/exclusive boundary. Recommend: inclusive (<=10km is eligible). Must be explicitly decided." },
    { "scenario": "No express-qualified drivers (4.0+ stars) are available at order placement time", "expected_behavior": "GAP — spec doesn't address this. Options: (A) reject express at checkout, (B) accept and hope a driver becomes available within 15 min. Recommend: show express as unavailable if no qualified drivers are in range." },
    { "scenario": "10km distance measurement method: straight-line vs driving distance", "expected_behavior": "GAP — 10km straight-line could be 15km driving. Must be explicitly decided. Recommend: driving distance, as it reflects actual fulfillment feasibility." },
    { "scenario": "Customer provides a partially wrong address (e.g., wrong apartment number but correct building)", "expected_behavior": "AMBIGUOUS — 'wrong address' is not precisely defined. Need a clear taxonomy of what constitutes customer address fault vs minor correction." },
    { "scenario": "Driver picks up at minute 14, but delivery completes at 2:03:00", "expected_behavior": "Pickup SLA was technically met, but delivery SLA was missed. Refund adjudication should apply since the overall 2-hour window was breached." },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels, replacement assigned at minute 9, but the 2-hour window is now infeasible due to the delay", "expected_behavior": "GAP — spec doesn't say whether the customer should be proactively notified that the window is likely to be missed, or whether to offer a preemptive refund." },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouses within 10km — which one is the order fulfilled from?", "expected_behavior": "GAP — spec assumes a single warehouse proximity check. Need rules for warehouse selection (closest, best-stocked, fastest estimated fulfillment)." },
    { "scenario": "Express order placed at 11:50 PM — 2-hour window spans midnight and potentially crosses into non-operating hours", "expected_behavior": "GAP — no operating hours defined for express. Does the 2-hour clock pause outside operating hours? Are there blackout periods?" },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order is placed", "expected_behavior": "GAP — does the 10km eligibility re-check? Does the 2-hour clock reset? What if the new address is >10km?" },
    { "scenario": "Automatic refund (BR-9) is triggered but the customer also had a wrong address", "expected_behavior": "CONTRADICTION — BR-6 says no refund for wrong address, but BR-9 says auto-refund if no replacement in 10 min. Which rule wins? Need explicit priority." },
    { "scenario": "SLA-miss refund queued but Finance's 3 business day window vs CS's 'automatic' expectation", "expected_behavior": "See escalation E-2. 'Automatic' triggering of refund vs 3 business day processing must be reconciled." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) represents incremental revenue per order. Refund liability is significant if SLA miss rates are high — each miss is a full margin loss on the express surcharge plus processing cost. The auto-refund on driver cancellation (BR-9) creates an uncapped financial exposure if driver cancellation rates are not controlled. No volume projections or miss-rate targets were provided.",
    "operational": "The 15-minute pickup SLA and 4-star driver requirement significantly constrain the eligible driver pool. This creates a capacity bottleneck — express orders compete for a subset of top-rated drivers who must also be near a warehouse. Real-time tracking adds infrastructure cost (WebSocket/SSE, location ingestion pipeline). The 10-minute replacement window requires a fast re-matching system, likely a dedicated express dispatch queue.",
    "user": "Strong value proposition — customers get speed, transparency (real-time tracking), and a refund safety net. However, the Legal constraint ('best effort') directly undermines the PM's 'guaranteed' language. If marketing says 'guaranteed' but ToS says 'best effort,' customer trust erodes on the first miss. The wrong-address exclusion will generate CS friction — customers may dispute what constitutes 'wrong.'",
    "risk": "HIGH — the guarantee-vs-best-effort contradiction (E-1) is a brand and legal liability. If the product launches with 'guaranteed' messaging but Legal has only approved 'best effort,' the company is exposed to false advertising complaints and regulatory risk. Operationally, the constrained driver pool could cause frequent SLA misses at launch, triggering a refund spiral.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Primary beneficiaries of faster delivery, but confused if 'guarantee' language doesn't match refund experience. Wrong-address disputes will create friction." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Top-rated drivers get more express assignments (higher utilization, possible fatigue). Sub-4.0 drivers are excluded from express revenue. Rating becomes higher-stakes." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Ops", "impact": "Must support 15-minute pickup readiness, likely requiring pre-staging or priority picking for express orders." },
      { "group": "Finance", "impact": "Owns refund processing within 3 business days. Must build or adapt refund workflows. Needs visibility into auto-refund triggers to forecast liability." },
      { "group": "Customer Success", "impact": "Will handle disputes around wrong-address exclusions, SLA miss communications, and the gap between 'guaranteed' marketing and 'best effort' reality." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must approve all customer-facing language. 'Guaranteed' wording is currently unapproved. Refund policy framing as 'goodwill' needs clear ToS language." },
      { "group": "Engineering", "impact": "Must build real-time tracking, SLA monitoring, auto-refund triggers, driver eligibility filtering, and the 10-minute replacement workflow." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 38,
    "reasoning": "The spec contains a fundamental contradiction (guarantee vs best effort) that affects the entire product's value proposition and legal standing. Additionally, there are multiple unresolved gaps (distance measurement, operating hours, driver availability at checkout, wrong address definition) and a stakeholder conflict between CS's 'automatic refund' and Finance's '3 business day' processing. Until the guarantee contradiction is resolved by PM and Legal jointly, the product cannot be correctly specified.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) is clearly stated",
      "10km eligibility radius is agreed upon (though measurement method is not)",
      "4-star driver minimum is a clear operational rule",
      "Base delivery fee is excluded from SLA-miss refunds",
      "Wrong address disqualifies refund"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — this changes everything downstream",
      "How 'automatic' the refund actually is vs 3 business day processing",
      "Definition of 'wrong address' for refund exclusion",
      "What happens when no qualified drivers are available",
      "Operating hours and blackout periods for express",
      "Distance measurement method (straight-line vs driving)"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 2-hour window is measured from order placement time, not from pickup",
      "The 10km radius is measured from the customer's delivery address to the nearest warehouse",
      "Driver rating is the cumulative platform rating, not a per-period rating",
      "Only one warehouse fulfills a given express order",
      "The replacement driver in BR-8/BR-9 must also meet the 4-star minimum"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "PM states the 2-hour delivery is 'guaranteed.' Legal explicitly states it is 'best effort' and not a contractual guarantee. These are mutually exclusive positions that affect every downstream decision: customer-facing language, refund policy framing, liability exposure, and marketing materials. This is the single most critical issue blocking a correct domain model.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Legal/Compliance", "Marketing", "Customer Success", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Adopt Legal's position — 'best effort' SLA with goodwill refund. Update all PM/CS language accordingly.",
        "B: Adopt PM's position — contractual guarantee. Legal must approve enforceable guarantee language and accept liability.",
        "C: Hybrid — market as 'Express Priority' without the word 'guarantee,' offer the refund as a branded promise (e.g., 'Express Promise') that is goodwill but feels like a guarantee to customers."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C. It satisfies Legal's constraint while preserving PM's customer value proposition. But this MUST be a joint PM-Legal decision, not an engineering or domain modeling call."
    },
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "Customer Success says refund should be 'automatic' on replacement driver failure (implying instant). Finance says refunds are 'processed within 3 business days.' These are incompatible if 'automatic' means instant credit. Need to clarify: does 'automatic' mean 'triggered without manual approval' (but still processed in Finance's 3-day window), or does it mean 'instant credit to customer'?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customer Success", "Finance", "Customers", "Engineering"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-triggered, 3-day processing — refund is created automatically but Finance processes it in their normal cycle.",
        "B: Instant credit — system immediately credits the customer, Finance reconciles after the fact.",
        "C: Tiered — instant credit for small amounts (like $12.99), batch processing for larger refunds."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A is safest. 'Automatic' means no human approval gate, but processing follows Finance's existing 3-day SLA. Communicate to customer: 'Your refund has been approved and will appear within 3 business days.'"
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The 10km eligibility radius has no defined measurement method. Straight-line (haversine) distance vs actual driving distance can differ by 30-50% in urban areas. A customer 10km straight-line could be 15km driving, making the 2-hour window infeasible.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Operations", "Engineering", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Straight-line distance — simpler to compute, but overpromises feasibility.",
        "B: Driving distance — accurate but requires routing API calls at checkout time.",
        "C: Conservative straight-line (e.g., 7km straight-line ≈ 10km driving) — simple but reduces eligible area."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B (driving distance) for correctness. The 10km threshold exists to protect the 2-hour SLA; using an inaccurate proxy defeats its purpose."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "'Wrong address' is not defined. Is it a completely invalid address? A valid address but wrong recipient location? A missing apartment number? A typo in street name that the driver eventually resolves? This is the basis for refund denial (BR-6) and will be disputed by customers.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customer Success", "Legal/Compliance", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Strict — any address discrepancy flagged by the driver counts as 'wrong.'",
        "B: Lenient — only addresses that require re-routing or are undeliverable count.",
        "C: Tiered with driver attestation — driver flags the issue, CS reviews before denying refund."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C. Wrong-address refund denial will be contentious. Require driver attestation AND CS review before denying a refund. This protects the customer relationship while preventing abuse."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No operating hours, blackout periods, or capacity limits are defined for express delivery. Can a customer order express at 2 AM? On holidays? When there's a surge? Without this, the system cannot correctly validate express eligibility.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Operations", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: 24/7 availability with dynamic driver pool.",
        "B: Defined operating hours (e.g., 8 AM - 10 PM).",
        "C: Dynamic availability based on real-time driver supply."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C with Option B as a floor. Express is only available during defined hours AND when qualified driver supply meets a minimum threshold."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No specification for what happens when express is selected but no 4-star+ drivers are available. The order could be accepted (risking SLA miss) or rejected (losing revenue). This is a critical order-time decision.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product Management", "Operations", "Customers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Reject express at checkout if no qualified driver is available within range.",
        "B: Accept and attempt assignment, escalating if 15-min pickup SLA is missed.",
        "C: Show estimated availability ('Express available in ~20 min') and let customer decide."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A. Accepting an express order with no available driver virtually guarantees an SLA miss and a refund. Better to be honest at checkout."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "Cannot define a correct domain model while the guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is unresolved", "business_rule_broken": "The entire refund and SLA framework depends on whether this is a guarantee or best-effort. BR-5 and BR-9 have different legal and operational meanings under each interpretation." },
    { "violation": "Auto-refund trigger conflicts with Finance processing SLA", "business_rule_broken": "BR-7 (3 business days) and BR-9 (automatic refund) are not reconciled. Engineering cannot implement both without knowing which 'automatic' means." }
  ]
}
